title: Velvety Korean Rice Wrap
description: Floral, pillowy wraps filled with silky tofu and crisp veggies, finished with a glossy caramel drizzle for a festive vegetarian treat.
instructions: Rinse the rice until water runs clear, then cook with 1.5 cups water in a rice cooker until fluffy.
While rice cooks, steam the napa cabbage leaves for 30 seconds, then rinse under cool water and pat dry.
In a bowl, mix silken tofu, julienned carrot, cucumber, and sliced green onion.
In a small saucepan, melt butter over medium heat, then stir in sugar until dissolved and the mixture turns golden.
Add soy sauce and sesame oil to the caramel sauce, simmer for 2 minutes, then remove from heat.
Warm the rice in a microwave for 1 minute to make it easier to spread.
Place a spoonful of rice on a cabbage leaf, top with the tofu mixture, and drizzle a little caramel sauce over the filling.
Sprinkle sesame seeds, then fold the edges of the leaf over the rice and roll tightly to form a wrap.
Steam the wrapped bundles in a steamer basket for 5 minutes to seal the edges and warm the filling.
Drizzle the remaining caramel sauce over the finished wraps before serving.
Garnish with additional sesame seeds if desired, and serve immediately.
